name: Chronic heart failure
description: Chronic heart failure — long-standing impairment of the heart's pumping function.

en.name: Chronic heart failure
en.shortDescription: Chronic heart failure — long-standing impairment of the heart's pumping function.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Chronic heart failure is a long-standing impairment of the heart's ability to pump blood, causing breathlessness, fatigue and fluid retention. In intensive care it is a comorbidity affecting fluid management, weaning and prognosis, and a cause of admission when decompensated.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *444031 / 48447003 — Chronic heart failure* with all descendants included, capturing chronic heart failure and its subtypes.

## Mapping Notes

- Acute heart failure / acute decompensation is a distinct clinical event; this set captures the chronic condition.
- Heart failure with preserved versus reduced ejection fraction is not distinguished at the set level.
